renderer.c(3) Library Functions Manual renderer.c(3)

NAME

renderer.c - render_file_* — ties together font resolution, platform renderer, FFmpeg encoder

SYNOPSIS

Data Structures


struct render_file_ctx_s

Functions


asciichat_error_t render_file_create (const char *output_path, int cols, int rows, int fps, int theme, render_file_ctx_t **out)
void render_file_set_audio_source (render_file_ctx_t *ctx, void *audio_media_source, void *audio_capture_rb)
asciichat_error_t render_file_write_frame (render_file_ctx_t *ctx, const char *ansi_frame, uint64_t captured_ns)
void render_file_set_snapshot_actual_duration (render_file_ctx_t *ctx, double actual_duration_sec)
asciichat_error_t render_file_destroy (render_file_ctx_t *ctx)

Detailed Description

render_file_* — ties together font resolution, platform renderer, FFmpeg encoder

Definition in file renderer.c.

Function Documentation

asciichat_error_t render_file_create (const char * output_path, int cols, int rows, int fps, int theme, render_file_ctx_t ** out)

Definition at line 29 of file renderer.c.

30                                                               {
31   log_info("[RENDER_FILE_CREATE] CALLED with output_path=%s, cols=%d, rows=%d, fps=%d, theme=%d, out=%p",
32            output_path ? output_path : "(null)", cols, rows, fps, theme, (void *)out);
33   render_file_ctx_t *ctx = SAFE_CALLOC(1, sizeof(*ctx), render_file_ctx_t *);
34 
35   // Resolve font spec → platform-appropriate file path, family name, or bundled data.
36   char font_spec[512] = {0};
37   bool font_is_path = false;
38   const uint8_t *font_data = NULL;
39   size_t font_data_size = 0;
40   const char *raw_font = GET_OPTION(render_font);
41 
42   // Auto-select matrix font when --matrix flag is set, unless user explicitly overrides with --render-font
43   if ((!raw_font || raw_font[0] == '\0') && GET_OPTION(matrix_rain)) {
44     raw_font = "matrix";
45     log_debug("render_file_create: [MATRIX] Using matrix font (enabled by --matrix flag)");
46   }
47 
48   // BEFORE resolving fonts, check if matrix font and disable it for render-file (causes glyph height=0)
49   if (raw_font && strcmp(raw_font, "matrix") == 0) {
50     log_warn("render_file_create: [MATRIX] Matrix font not supported for render-file output, using system font instead");
51     raw_font = NULL;  // Force fallback to system font
52   }
53 
54   asciichat_error_t fe =
55       platform_font_resolve(raw_font, font_spec, sizeof(font_spec), &font_is_path, &font_data, &font_data_size);
56   if (fe != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
57     log_warn("renderer: font resolution failed for '%s' — using system default", raw_font ? raw_font : "(explicit system)");
58   }
59 
60   log_debug("render_file_create: Font resolved: font_spec='%s', font_is_path=%d, font_data=%p (size=%zu)", font_spec,
61             font_is_path, (void *)font_data, font_data_size);
62 
63   // Use reasonable default font size for render-file quality
64   double font_size_pt = GET_OPTION(render_font_size);
65 
66   term_renderer_config_t tr_cfg = {
67       .cols = cols,
68       .rows = rows,
69       .font_size_pt = font_size_pt,
70       .theme = (term_renderer_theme_t)theme,
71       .font_is_path = font_is_path,
72       .font_data = font_data,
73       .font_data_size = font_data_size,
74   };
75   strncpy(tr_cfg.font_spec, font_spec, sizeof(tr_cfg.font_spec) - 1);
76 
77   asciichat_error_t err = term_renderer_create(&tr_cfg, &ctx->renderer);
78   if (err != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
79     log_error("render_file_create: term_renderer_create failed: %s", asciichat_error_string(err));
80     SAFE_FREE(ctx);
81     return err;
82   }
83   int actual_width_px = term_renderer_width_px(ctx->renderer);
84   int actual_height_px = term_renderer_height_px(ctx->renderer);
85   log_info("render_file_create: GRID DIMENSIONS: %ux%u cells -> PIXEL DIMENSIONS: %dx%d px", cols, rows,
86            actual_width_px, actual_height_px);
87   log_debug("render_file_create: term_renderer created (%dx%d cells, %dx%d px)", cols, rows, actual_width_px,
88             actual_height_px);
89 
90   err = ffmpeg_encoder_create(output_path, actual_width_px, actual_height_px, fps, &ctx->encoder);
91   if (err != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
92     log_error("render_file_create: ffmpeg_encoder_create failed: %s", asciichat_error_string(err));
93     term_renderer_destroy(ctx->renderer);
94     SAFE_FREE(ctx);
95     return err;
96   }
97   log_debug("render_file_create: ffmpeg_encoder created successfully");
98 
99   // Initialize audio fields
100   ctx->audio_sample_rate = 48000;  // Audio pipeline is 48kHz
101   // Allocate large buffer for snapshot mode (5 seconds at 48kHz with ~14 FPS = 3,428 samples per frame)
102   // Use 8192 to comfortably handle snapshot reads without excessive overhead
103   ctx->audio_buf_size = 8192;      // Temporary buffer for reading audio (in floats)
104   ctx->audio_read_buf = SAFE_MALLOC(ctx->audio_buf_size * sizeof(float), float *);
105   ctx->audio_media_source = NULL;
106   ctx->audio_capture_rb = NULL;
107 
108   log_info("renderer: initialized encoder for %s", output_path);
109 
110   // Register render file context for debugging
111   NAMED_REGISTER_CONTEXT(ctx, "render_file_ctx", output_path, NULL);
112 
113   *out = ctx;
114   return ASCIICHAT_OK;
115 }

    render_file_ctx_s::audio_buf_size,

asciichat_error_t render_file_destroy
  (render_file_ctx_t * ctx)


Definition at line 311 of file renderer.c.


311                                                               {
312   if (!ctx)
313     return ASCIICHAT_OK;
314   asciichat_error_t err = ffmpeg_encoder_destroy(ctx->encoder);
315   term_renderer_destroy(ctx->renderer);
316   SAFE_FREE(ctx->audio_read_buf);
317   SAFE_FREE(ctx);
318   return err;
319 }

asciichat_error_t render_file_write_frame
  (render_file_ctx_t * ctx, const char * ansi_frame, uint64_t
  captured_ns)


Definition at line 124 of file renderer.c.


124                                                                                                                 {
125   // Write to debug file directly to bypass logging system
126   FILE *dbg = fopen("/tmp/render-debug.txt", "a");
127   if (dbg) {
128     fprintf(dbg, "[RENDER_WRITE_FRAME] Called with ctx=%p, frame_len=%zu, captured_ns=%llu\n", (void *)ctx,
129             ansi_frame ? strlen(ansi_frame) : 0, (unsigned long long)captured_ns);
130     fclose(dbg);
131   }
132   log_info("render_file_write_frame: CALLED - ctx=%p, captured_ns=%llu", (void *)ctx, (unsigned long long)captured_ns);
133 
134   if (!ctx) {
135     log_warn("render_file_write_frame: ctx is NULL");
136     return ASCIICHAT_OK;
137   }
138 
139   if (!ansi_frame) {
140     log_warn("render_file_write_frame: ansi_frame is NULL");
141     return ASCIICHAT_OK;
142   }
143 
144   size_t frame_len = strlen(ansi_frame);
145   log_info("render_file_write_frame: processing frame (len=%zu)", frame_len);
146   if (frame_len > 0) {
147     log_info("  first 100 chars: %.100s", ansi_frame);
148   }
149 
150   asciichat_error_t err = term_renderer_feed(ctx->renderer, ansi_frame, frame_len);
151   if (err != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
152     log_warn("render_file_write_frame: term_renderer_feed failed: %s", asciichat_error_string(err));
153     return err;
154   }
155 
156   const uint8_t *pixels = term_renderer_pixels(ctx->renderer);
157   int pitch = term_renderer_pitch(ctx->renderer);
158   int width_px = term_renderer_width_px(ctx->renderer);
159   int height_px = term_renderer_height_px(ctx->renderer);
160 
161   log_info("render_file_write_frame: pixels=%p pitch=%d dims=%dx%d", (void *)pixels, pitch, width_px, height_px);
162 
163   // Check pixels in multiple locations to verify content
164   if (pixels) {
165     // Check pixel at (0,0) - top left corner
166     uint8_t sample_r = pixels[0], sample_g = pixels[1], sample_b = pixels[2];
167 
168     // Check pixel at (500, 100) where we injected a test red stripe
169     size_t test_offset = 100 * pitch + 500 * 3;
170     uint8_t test_r = pixels[test_offset], test_g = pixels[test_offset + 1], test_b = pixels[test_offset + 2];
171 
172     // Check pixel at middle of screen
173     uint8_t sample_r_mid = pixels[(height_px / 2) * pitch], sample_g_mid = pixels[(height_px / 2) * pitch + 1],
174             sample_b_mid = pixels[(height_px / 2) * pitch + 2];
175 
176     log_info("  pixel[0,0]: RGB(%u,%u,%u), test_stripe[500,100]: RGB(%u,%u,%u), pixel[0,%d]: RGB(%u,%u,%u)",
177              sample_r, sample_g, sample_b, test_r, test_g, test_b, height_px/2, sample_r_mid, sample_g_mid, sample_b_mid);
178   }
179 
180   // CRITICAL: Copy pixel buffer before encoding
181   // term_renderer_pixels() returns a pointer to the renderer's internal buffer which gets
182   // overwritten on the next frame. We must copy the data before passing to the encoder.
183   uint8_t *pixels_copy = NULL;
184   if (pixels) {
185     size_t pixel_buffer_size = (size_t)pitch * (size_t)height_px;
186     pixels_copy = SAFE_MALLOC(pixel_buffer_size, uint8_t *);
187     memcpy(pixels_copy, pixels, pixel_buffer_size);
188     log_info("render_file_write_frame: copied %zu bytes of pixel data (was at %p, now at %p)",
189              pixel_buffer_size, (void *)pixels, (void *)pixels_copy);
190 
191     // DEBUG: Inject test red stripe to verify encoder is working
192     static int frame_num = 0;
193     if (frame_num++ < 1) {
194       log_info("DEBUG: Injecting test RED vertical stripe into pixel buffer for verification");
195       // Fill a 100-pixel-wide vertical stripe with bright red at x=500
196       for (int y = 0; y < height_px; y++) {
197         for (int x = 500; x < 600 && x < width_px; x++) {
198           uint8_t *pixel = pixels_copy + y * pitch + x * 3;
199           pixel[0] = 255;  // R
200           pixel[1] = 0;    // G
201           pixel[2] = 0;    // B
202         }
203       }
204     }
205   }
206 
207   err = ffmpeg_encoder_write_frame(ctx->encoder, pixels_copy, pitch, captured_ns);
208   if (err != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
209     log_warn("render_file_write_frame: ffmpeg_encoder_write_frame failed: %s", asciichat_error_string(err));
210   }
211 
212   // Free the pixel copy (ffmpeg_encoder_write_frame reads and converts it immediately)
213   SAFE_FREE(pixels_copy);
214 
215   // Write synchronized audio for this frame if available
216   // In snapshot mode, track actual FPS and calculate samples_per_frame dynamically
217   if (ctx && ctx->audio_read_buf && ctx->encoder) {
218     // Detect if we're in snapshot mode
219     bool snapshot_mode = GET_OPTION(snapshot_mode);
220     double snapshot_delay = GET_OPTION(snapshot_delay);
221 
222     int samples_per_frame;
223 
224     // Track frame timing for dynamic FPS calculation in snapshot mode
225     static uint64_t first_frame_ns = 0;
226     static int log_once = 0;
227 
228     if (first_frame_ns == 0) {
229       first_frame_ns = captured_ns;
230     }
231 
232     if (snapshot_mode && snapshot_delay > 0) {
233       // Simple approach: distribute snapshot_delay * sample_rate evenly across expected ~70 frames
234       // This ensures total audio duration = snapshot_delay seconds
235       int total_target_samples = (int)(ctx->audio_sample_rate * snapshot_delay);
236       int expected_frame_count = (int)(13.6 * snapshot_delay);  // 13.6 FPS is typical capture rate
237       samples_per_frame = total_target_samples / expected_frame_count;
238 
239       // Ensure minimum to avoid rounding to 0
240       if (samples_per_frame < 100) {
241         samples_per_frame = 100;
242       }
243 
244     } else {
245       // Normal/live mode: use 60 FPS baseline
246       samples_per_frame = ctx->audio_sample_rate / 60;
247       if (!log_once++) {
248         log_info("[AUDIO_CALC] Normal mode: samples_per_frame=%d", samples_per_frame);
249       }
250     }
251 
252     if (samples_per_frame > ctx->audio_buf_size) {
253       samples_per_frame = ctx->audio_buf_size;
254       if (!log_once++) {
255         log_warn("[AUDIO_CALC] samples_per_frame clamped to buffer size %d", ctx->audio_buf_size);
256       }
257     }
258     if (samples_per_frame <= 0) {
259       samples_per_frame = 800;  // Fallback minimum
260     }
261 
262     int samples_read = 0;
263     static int audio_eof_reached = 0;
264 
265     // Read from whichever audio source is available (prefer media source, fallback to capture)
266     if (!audio_eof_reached && ctx->audio_media_source) {
267       // Read from media source (file/URL audio) - try to get samples
268       samples_read = media_source_read_audio(ctx->audio_media_source, ctx->audio_read_buf, samples_per_frame);
269 
270       if (samples_read <= 0) {
271         // EOF reached or error - no more audio samples available from source
272         audio_eof_reached = 1;
273         samples_read = 0;
274       }
275     } else if (!audio_eof_reached && ctx->audio_capture_rb) {
276       // Read from ring buffer (live mic capture)
277       samples_read =
278           audio_ring_buffer_read(ctx->audio_capture_rb, ctx->audio_read_buf, samples_per_frame);
279       if (samples_read < 0) {
280         samples_read = 0;  // On error, write silence
281         audio_eof_reached = 1;
282       } else if (samples_read == 0) {
283         audio_eof_reached = 1;
284       }
285       log_debug("render_file_write_frame: reading audio from capture_rb (samples_per_frame=%d, read=%d)",
286                 samples_per_frame, samples_read);
287     }
288 
289     // Write audio: either from source or silence to fill remaining time
290     if (samples_read > 0) {
291       // We got samples from the source - write them
292       ffmpeg_encoder_write_audio(ctx->encoder, ctx->audio_read_buf, samples_read);
293     } else {
294       // No samples available (EOF reached or not available) - write silence to maintain sync
295       // This ensures audio track duration matches video track duration
296       memset(ctx->audio_read_buf, 0, samples_per_frame * sizeof(float));
297       ffmpeg_encoder_write_audio(ctx->encoder, ctx->audio_read_buf, samples_per_frame);
298     }
299 
300   }
301 
302   return err;
303 }

    render_file_ctx_s::audio_buf_size,
    render_file_ctx_s::audio_capture_rb,
    render_file_ctx_s::audio_media_source,
    render_file_ctx_s::audio_read_buf, audio_ring_buffer_read(),
    render_file_ctx_s::audio_sample_rate,
    render_file_ctx_s::encoder, ffmpeg_encoder_write_audio(),
    ffmpeg_encoder_write_frame(), GET_OPTION, log_debug,
    log_info, log_once, log_warn,
    media_source_read_audio(), render_file_ctx_s::renderer,
    SAFE_FREE, SAFE_MALLOC, term_renderer_feed(),
    term_renderer_height_px(), term_renderer_pitch(),
    term_renderer_pixels(), and term_renderer_width_px().
